title: test/system: make some tests faster part 3
body:

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/lgtm

Thanks for doing this. It seems like a lot of effort, I hope the gains are worth it.

/hold
Do not merge or restart tests, a changed test flaked https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6270902646603776/html/sys-podman-fedora-39-root-host-boltdb.log.html

I take a look tomorrow at the cause.
Yeah, I was just looking at that. Failure does not make sense to me.

> Yeah, I was just looking at that. Failure does not make sense to me.

Given the test took 10s I think the issue is that the container unit got stop before it installed the sigterm handler. I think the fix is to do the trap before the unit sends the ready message.
rebased + changed the "quadlet - pod simple" and "quadlet - basic" to do the trap before the ready message. Hopefully that is enough.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
... still flaking for some reason, I don't get it.

/hold cancel
I think this is good to go, the issue was the missing `Notify=yes` in the container unit this socat was failing and because the exit command in the trap uses the last exit code it made the main process fail as long as the sleep 0.1 did not finish before we stopped it.
